title: Native support of Windows Containers for Podman for Windows without relying on a VM
body:
### Feature request description

On a Windows only environnement, Podman for Windows and Podman Desktop for Windows should be able to be used to work with Windows Containers without relying on any utility VM (podman machine).

Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/virtualization/windowscontainers/

### Suggest potential solution

Direct use of Windows API for managing Windows containers and if possible without relying on any docker dependency. 

- Host Networking Service (HNS) : https://github.com/microsoft/hnslib
- Host Compute Service (HCS) : https://github.com/microsoft/hcsshim

Via containerd running locally on the Windows host, it should possible to have the interface with HCS, see runhcs -https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/virtualization/windowscontainers/deploy-containers/containerd

### Have you considered any alternatives?

https://www.tunbury.org/2025/06/11/windows-containerd/

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: The Podman project has no plans to support native Windows containers.

> The Podman project has no plans to support native Windows containers.

It's somehow surprising because podman clearly target support of OCI containers that include support of OS type as linux, freebsd, windows, wasm, mixte.

Here we can take the assumption that the host OS is the same as the OS type of the containers. 

AFAIK, the is already a podman support of FreeBSD containers on FreeBSD.

Cross architecture (emulation and/or os type) issues stay distinct questions apart.

FreeBSD support was added by the FreeBSD maintainers, and we were happy to review and merge the changes. We have no intention of working on native Windows container support (in particular, as the amount of changes required to get things like Libpod to build on a non-Unix operating system will be very, very large), but if someone else was to step up and do the work, I think we'd be happy to review and merge.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
ok so @mheon i like to contribute for the windows native support for podman, so can i start with that one??
If you'd like to take a stab at it, feel free.

Any large work should have a proper design on how this should work, see our new design process https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/contrib/design-docs

This is not a once and done task, this will require a ton of work. How to store container images on windows, i.e. support for a windows storage driver in the storage lib? How to do networking, i.e. netavark support? How does the container monitoring work with conmon? How should maintenance work long term?
Freebsd is already painful to deal with due all the _linux.go _freebsd.go files that night slightly different implementations. With windows this would get so much worse there. Lack of support from the critical security relevant libraries such as https://github.com/cyphar/filepath-securejoin is a major issue for us maintenance wise for example.

I am not saying it cannot be done or that I would reject it outright but any work in this area cannot be just a one time thing. It would require significant convincing to me that that person is going to stick around as active mainliner to maintain the windows port.

@Luap99 i know the complexity of this thing, i think we should make another branch or repo or whatever a maintainer suggests

even it will require working with podman desktop too, making the changes for windows on the same tree will clutter things as it is what i see, kindly help me deciding the architecture
